title: Cryptographic Hashes, SHA-2, SHA-3, and Commitment Boundaries · Language Lens: Crypto Go Weak Hash Migration
summary: A download service calls SHA-256 encryption, truncates digests without analysis, uses MD5 for adversarial integrity, and hashes ambiguous JSON. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the weak hash migration increment in hash-purpose and digest verification contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence d…
objectives: Confine MD5 and SHA-1 to explicitly non-adversarial compatibility or migration checks and reject security claims based on them.
Hash bounded streams incrementally, propagate read errors, freeze canonical byte identity, and compare expected digests safely.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Weak Hash Migration · Theory Crypto Go Weak Hash Migration
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 3864 during a replicated device restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to select a hash by purpose while rejecting weak algorithms, ambiguous bytes, and unauthenticated digest claims; preserve accessible status, non-color meaning, keyboard operation, error recovery, and secret-safe diagnostics; then swap the role, tenant, recipient, algorithm, or context and prove domain separation. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
